{{Quote|I think you're a dangerous and foolhardy man.|[[Ovolot Qail Uthan]]|<ref name="Republic Commando: Hard Contact">''[[Republic Commando: Hard Contact]]''</ref>}}
'''Ghez Hokan''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] who rented his services to the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Biography==
Some time before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] Ghez Hokan found work as the primary enforcer for [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] [[Lik Ankkit]], the [[Trade Federation of Planets|Trade Federation's]] representative on the remote [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Qiilura/Legends|Qiilura]]. He wasn't fond of the job, however, as his own men gave him more trouble than the share-croppers he terrorized. The dregs of the underworld, his 'army' was composed of [[Weequay/Legends|Weequays]], [[Ubese/Legends|Ubese]], and whoever else his employer recruited, and the mercenary despised them. Hokan personally [[Death/Legends|killed]] Jedi Master [[Kast Fulier]] with the latter's own [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], which Hokan kept because it made a convenient tool for executing lieutenants. When the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]] built a top-secret research facility on Qiilura, the mercenary jumped at the chance for a career change.<ref name="Republic Commando: Hard Contact" />

Hokan made a deal with the Confederacy that cut Ankkit out of the picture and gave him military control of the planet, with him being assigned the provisional rank of major. He was assigned by the Separatists to protect the bioweaponry research bunker on Qiilura and more specifically, [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Ovolot Qail Uthan]] and her research team. When [[Omega Squad]] arrived on the planet, the Confederacy supplemented Hokan's militia with one hundred [[B1-series battle droid|B1 battle droids]] and a handful of organic lieutenants. Hokan led the search for Omega Squad, not knowing he was facing only four men until it was too late.<ref name="Republic Commando: Hard Contact" />

Fulier's [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Etain Tur-Mukan]], managed to escape Hokan, and was on the run when she met a sole member of the newly joined Omega Squad. She was horribly upset at her Jedi Master's death, and immediately tried to kill the clone, mistaking the clone for Hokan because the commando's Katarn armor was visually similar to Mandalorian armor, despite the difference in paint schemes. When Omega Squad destroyed the clone-killing virus and captured Dr. Uthan, [[Atin Skirata|Atin]] and [[Darman Skirata|Darman]] brought both back to a waiting [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/Legends|LAAT]]/i [[Gunship/Legends|gunship]], but Hokan's second in command came with a [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] and shot Atin with a high-powered [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]] [[Verpine shatter gun|weapon]].<ref name="Republic Commando: Hard Contact" />

Meanwhile, [[Niner Skirata|Niner]] and [[Fi Skirata|Fi]] faced Hokan by themselves. Niner tricked Hokan by lying on the ground and pretended he was horribly wounded, screaming not for his mother, like most organic men, but for his training sergeant, [[Kal Skirata]]. Hokan was about to kill Niner with a vibroblade out of pity, but [[Etain Tur-Mukan]] attacked Hokan from behind and decapitated him.<ref name="Republic Commando: Hard Contact" /> Fi kept Hokan's armor as a souvenir, and wore it as his own when later living on [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]. Uthan, seeing Hokan's armor, thought he had survived when she too was brought to Mandalore by Fi's father, [[Kal Skirata]], but was relieved when she realized it was only Fi in his armor.<ref name="Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel">''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|'''Major''' Hokan, please. If you see any of my former employees wandering around, don't shelter them, will you?... I'd like to handle their outplacement package personally.|The newly promoted Ghez Hokan expressing his intent to murder his disobedient colleagues|<ref name="Republic Commando: Hard Contact" />}}
Hokan was seen by many as an unpleasantly violent figure, and was rumored to have been kicked out of Death Watch for "enjoying his work too much." He was ruthless and efficient, seen in when he would kill off his former militia for being ineffective against the clone squads assaulting Dr. Uthan's lab.<ref name="Republic Commando: Hard Contact" />

Conversely, Ghez Hoken showed that he was highly appreciative of those who proved themselves valuable assets. When the [[Umbaran]] mercenary Lieutenant [[Hurati]] showed himself to be far more dependable than his other officers in the hunt for Omega Squad, he promised Hurati a promotion to the rank of CIS [[Captain/Legends|Captain]]. Furthermore, after Ghez Hoken was trapped behind a blastdoor that was sabotaged by the commando named [[Atin Skirata|Atin]], Hurati was able to [[Slicer/Legends|slice]] into the door controls to save him, leading the Mandalorian to claim he loved Hurati more than if he was his own son.

He also had some degree of honor, when he refused to use a lightsaber to strike down a clone of Jango Fett, as he thought it would be degrading. Hokan also ensured that after he had killed his Ubese mercenary associate [[Cailshh]], that another Ubese mercenary had Cailshh's corpse tended to in accordance to proper Ubese funeral practices, showing that while he had no tolerance for incompetence, he had a degree of respect for cultural funeral rites.<ref name="Republic Commando: Hard Contact" />
